href="https://github.com/Stedi/openApi/blob/main/healthcare.json#:~:text=&quot;operationId&quot;%3A%20&quot;CoordinationOfBenefits&quot;" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon text-tertiary"><path d="M4 18V14.3C4 13.4716 3.32843 12.8 2.5 12.8H2V11.2H2.5C3.32843 11.2 4 10.5284 4 9.7V6C4 4.34315 5.34315 3 7 3H8V5H7C6.44772 5 6 5.44772 6 6V10.1C6 10.9858 5.42408 11.7372 4.62623 12C5.42408 12.2628 6 13.0142 6 13.9V18C6 18.5523 6.44772 19 7 19H8V21H7C5.34315 21 4 19.6569 4 18ZM20 14.3V18C20 19.6569 18.6569 21 17 21H16V19H17C17.5523 19 18 18.5523 18 18V13.9C18 13.0142 18.5759 12.2628 19.3738 12C18.5759 11.7372 18 10.9858 18 10.1V6C18 5.44772 17.5523 5 17 5H16V3H17C18.6569 3 20 4.34315 20 6V9.7C20 10.5284 20.6716 11.2 21.5 11.2H22V12.8H21.5C20.6716 12.8 20 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>OpenAPI spec</a></div></div></div><p>Coordination of benefits (COB) checks help you determine whether patients are covered by multiple health plans, whether coverage overlap requires coordination of benefits, and each payer&#x27;s responsibility for payment (primacy).</p>
<ol>
<li>Call this endpoint with a JSON payload. Each check <strong>must</strong> be for a Stedi-supported payer with which the patient has coverage. Visit the <a href="https://www.stedi.com/healthcare/network?filter=eyJ0cmFuc2FjdGlvbkZpbHRlcnMiOnsiMjcwIjp7fSwiMjc2Ijp7fSwiODM1Ijp7fSwiODM3UCI6e30sIjgzN0kiOnt9LCI4MzdEIjp7fSwiQ09CIjp7ImlzU3VwcG9ydGVkIjp0cnVlfSwiMjc1VSI6e319LCJjb3ZlcmFnZVR5cGVzIjpbXX0%3D" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Payer Network</a> for a complete list of supported payers.</li>
<li>Stedi searches a national database containing 245+ million patient coverage records from 45+ health plans, ASOs, TPAs, and others, including participation from the vast majority of national commercial health plans. Data is updated weekly to ensure accuracy.</li>
<li>The endpoint returns information about the patient&#x27;s active health plans, the responsibility sequence number for each payer if available (such as primary or secondary), and whether coordination of benefits is required.</li>
</ol><div class="flex items-end justify-between gap-2"><h2 class="flex scroll-m-28 flex-row items-center gap-2 group api-section-heading-title" depth="2" slug="authorizations" id="authorizations"><a 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>Object</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>A dependent for which you want to check coordination of benefits.</p>
<ul>
<li>An individual qualifies as a dependent when they are listed as a dependent on the subscriber&#x27;s insurance plan AND the payer cannot uniquely identify them through information outside the subscriber&#x27;s policy. For example, if the dependent has their own member ID number, you should identify them in the <code>subscriber</code> object instead.</li>
<li>The demographic information you provide <strong>must</strong> patch the payer&#x27;s data exactly. For example, if the payer has the dependent&#x27;s name as <code>Jonathan Doe</code>, a COB request for <code>Jon Doe</code> will fail because the name doesn&#x27;t match the payer&#x27;s records.</li>
</ul><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" data-path="body.dependent" 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>Object</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-950/50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 whitespace-nowrap break-all" data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Information about the encounter.</p>
<ul>
<li>You can submit COB checks with the <code>30</code> service type code for Health Benefit Plan Coverage. This is the broadest service type code that covers all medical services and subtypes included in the patient’s health plan.</li>
<li>The service dates you provide <strong>must</strong> be within the past 2 years. COB checks don&#x27;t support requests with dates outside of this range.</li>
<li>Don&#x27;t send service dates that are in the future. Future service dates typically result in errors from the payer.</li>
<li>If you don&#x27;t specify a service date (either a single day or a range of dates), Stedi defaults to using the current date.</li>
</ul><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" data-path="body.encounter" data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>An object containing information about the entity requesting the COB check. This may be an individual practitioner, a medical group, a hospital, or another type of healthcare provider.  You must provide the <code>organizationName</code> (if the entity is an organization) or <code>firstName</code> and <code>lastName</code> (if the provider is an individual). You must also provide the provider&#x27;s <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/national-provider-identifier" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">National Provider Identifier</a> (<code>npi</code>).</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>Object</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-950/50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 whitespace-nowrap break-all" data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The primary policyholder for the insurance plan <em>or</em> a dependent with a unique member ID. If a dependent has a unique member ID, include their information here and leave <code>dependent</code> empty.</p>
<p>The demographic information you provide <strong>must</strong> match the payer&#x27;s data exactly. For example, if the payer has the subscriber&#x27;s name as <code>Jonathan Doe</code>, a COB request for <code>Jon Doe</code> will fail because the name doesn&#x27;t match the payer&#x27;s records. Also note that:</p>
<ul>
<li>Any prefix on the member&#x27;s card is considered part of the <code>memberID</code> used for the search.</li>
<li>Mismatches in the <code>memberId</code> are one of the most common causes of <code>Member Not Found</code> errors. We strongly recommend first performing an <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/api-reference/post-healthcare-eligibility" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Eligibility Check</a> and using the <code>memberId</code> in the response to populate your COB check.</li>
<li>We recommend including the <code>ssn</code> property in addition to the <code>memberId</code> if possible. This allows Stedi to do an additional search for the patient when the <code>memberId</code> doesn&#x27;t return a match.</li>
<li>Stedi can identify coverage overlap for the same payer if the member ID differs between the two coverages.</li>
</ul><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" data-path="body.subscriber" 80</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The member ID for the subscriber&#x27;s insurance policy.</p>
<p>You must provide at least one of the <code>memberId</code> or <code>ssn</code> properties in the request. However, we recommend including both if possible. This allows Stedi to do an additional search for patient information when the <code>memberId</code> doesn&#x27;t return a match.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property <p>You must provide at least one of the <code>memberId</code> or <code>ssn</code> properties in the request. However, we recommend including both if possible. This allows Stedi to do an additional search for patient information when the <code>memberId</code> doesn&#x27;t return a match.</p></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t bg-transparent ring-1 ring-inset ring-neutral-300 dark:ring-neutral-700 text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300 break-all">Length:<!-- --> <!-- -->1 - 80</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>This is the Payer ID. Visit the <a href="https://www.stedi.com/healthcare/network" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Payer Network</a> for a complete list of supported payers for COB checks.
- Each check <strong>must</strong> be for a participating health plan for which the patient has coverage. For example, if the patient has coverage from Cigna and UnitedHealthcare, a COB check to Aetna will return an error.
- Medicare and Medicare Advantage plans aren&#x27;t supported. If you submit a COB check for a Medicare or Medicare Advantage plan, the request will fail with an <code>AAA</code> = <code>75</code> error (Subscriber/Insured Not Found).
- Ensure that you&#x27;re sending the request to the correct payer entity. For example, Blue Cross Blue Shield (BCBS) has multiple entities that operate in different states. If you send a request to the wrong entity, the request will fail with an <code>AAA</code> = <code>75</code> error (Subscriber/Insured Not Found).</p></div></div></div></div><div class="flex flex-col <ul>
<li>Active coverage with the health plan identified in the COB request</li>
<li>Coverage overlap (if it exists) with one or more payers</li>
<li>Payer primacy details (if Stedi was able to determine)</li>
<li>Benefits details, such as coverage dates and service types</li>

3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Objects</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Contains either information about another payer with which the patient has coverage or information about the subscriber associated with the additional health plan.</p>
<p>For example, if you submit a COB check for a dependent to Cigna and Stedi finds additional coverage through Aetna, the <code>benefitsRelatedEntity</code> would include subscriber details for the Aetna plan.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The identification number for the entity, qualified by the code in <code>entityIdentification</code>. The ID returned in this property is proprietary to our COB check product, so you can&#x27;t use it as the Payer ID for eligibility checks or other API requests to Stedi. It likely doesn&#x27;t match the Payer IDs listed in the <a href="https://www.stedi.com/healthcare/network" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Payer Network</a>.</p></div></div><div pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Identifies the type of entity. This can either be a type of payer or the subscriber associated with the COB coverage.</p>
<p>Note that Stedi uses <code>Payer</code> either when there is no COB instance or when Stedi is unable to determine primacy.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div></div><div class="flex flex-col max-h-[700px] overflow-y-auto"><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>Insured or Subscriber</code></div></div><div Payer</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>Secondary Payer</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The COB benefits information code.</p>
<p>Stedi returns entries with <code>benefitsInformation.code</code> = <code>1</code> for every active coverage type for each health plan.</p>
<p>Stedi returns entries with <code>benefitsInformation.code</code> = <code>R</code> when overlapping coverage exists within the date of service provided in the COB check.</p><div><section items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M8 7C5.23858 7 3 9.23858 3 12C3 14.7614 5.23858 17 8 5 12C5 10.3431 6.34315 9 8 9C9.65685 9 11 10.3431 11 12C11 13.6569 9.65685 15 8 15Z"></path></svg>Boolean</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>If set to <code>true</code>, the COB response contains benefits overlap. A benefits overlap indicates that the patient has active coverage from two or more payers for the same service type code, including the subtypes of medical coverage.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!p-0 items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The classification for the benefits that have been discovered in the COB response. Stedi returns one of the following values:
- <code>CobInstanceExistsPrimacyDetermined</code>: COB Instance Exists and Primacy was determined
- <code>CobInstanceExistsPrimacyUndetermined</code>: COB Instance Exists and Primacy was NOT determined
- <code>CoverageOverlapNoBenefitOverlap</code>: Coverage Overlap detected with no Benefit Overlap
- <code>CoverageOverlapExistsNotSubjectToCob</code>: Coverage Overlap exists and is not subject to COB
- <code>MemberFoundNoCob</code>: Member found, no COB found</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!p-0 [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!rounded-none [role=tabpanel]_details_&amp;:!border-t [role=tabpanel]_details_&amp;:!p-3" items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M8 7C5.23858 7 3 9.23858 3 12C3 14.7614 5.23858 17 8 17H16C18.7614 17 21 14.7614 21 12C21 9.23858 18.7614 7 16 7H8ZM8 5H16C19.866 5 23 8.13401 23 12C23 15.866 19.866 19 16 19H8C4.13401 19 1 15.866 1 12C1 8.13401 4.13401 5 8 5ZM8 15C6.34315 15 5 13.6569 5 12C5 10.3431 6.34315 9 8 9C9.65685 9 11 10.3431 11 12C11 13.6569 9.65685 15 8 15Z"></path></svg>Boolean</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>If set to <code>true</code>, the COB response contains a coverage overlap, meaning that the patient has active coverage with two or more payers during the service date submitted in the COB request.
- Coverage overlap can be for coverages from the same payer if the member ID is different between the two coverages.
- A coverage overlap is necessary for a COB instance to exist.
- A coverage overlap can exist without there being a COB instance if either of the two coverages is not subject to COB for any reason.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!p-0 [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!rounded-none 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>Object</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Dates associated with the patient&#x27;s health plan coverage. This information is used to determine their eligibility for benefits.</p>
<ul>
<li>The provided dates apply to every every benefit within the patient&#x27;s health plan unless specifically noted within a <code>benefitsInformation.benefitsDateInformation</code> object.</li>
<li>If the payer sends back date(s) that are different for the subscriber and dependents, Stedi includes only the dates for the dependent in this object and omits the subscriber&#x27;s date(s). Dependents can have different coverage dates than the subscriber due to qualifying life events, such as starting a new job or passing the age limit for coverage through their parent&#x27;s plan.</li>
</ul><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" <p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 primary policyholder for the insurance plan _or_ a dependent with a unique member ID. If a dependent has a unique member ID, include their information here and leave `dependent` empty.\n\n\n\nThe demographic information you provide **must** match the payer's data exactly. For example, if the payer has the subscriber's name as `Jonathan Doe`, a COB request for `Jon Doe` will fail because the name doesn't match the payer's records. Also note that:\n  - Any prefix on the member's card is considered part of the `memberID` used for the search.\n  - Mismatches in the `memberId` are one of the most common causes of `Member Not Found` errors. We strongly recommend first performing an [Eligibility Check](https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/api-reference/post-healthcare-eligibility) and using the `memberId` in the response to populate your COB check.\n  - We recommend including the `ssn` property in addition to the `memberId` if possible. about the patient's healthcare benefits, including:\\n- Active coverage with the health plan identified in the COB request\\n- Coverage overlap (if it exists) with one or more payers\\n- Payer primacy details (if Stedi was able to determine)\\n- Benefits details, such as coverage dates and service types\",\"items\":{\"properties\":{\"benefitsDateInformation\":{\"description\":\"Dates associated with the benefits. All properties may either be expressed as a single date. Dates listed only apply to the `benefitsInformation` object in which this `benefitsDateInformation` is provided.\",\"properties\":{\"benefitBegin\":{\"description\":\"The date the benefits begin.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"benefitEnd\":{\"description\":\"The date the benefits end.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"coordinationOfBenefits\":{\"description\":\"Date or date range used for coordination of benefits instance.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"periodEnd\":{\"description\":\"The end of the coverage overlap. Included when Stedi finds an instance of coverage overlap.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"periodStart\":{\"description\":\"The start of the coverage overlap Included when Stedi finds an instance of coverage overlap.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"planBegin\":{\"description\":\"Coverage start date. If multiple coverage start dates exist due to different start dates on various coverage/service types, this date applies to the medical coverage.\",\"type\":\"string\"}},\"type\":\"object\"},\"benefitsRelatedEntities\":{\"description\":\"Contains either information about another payer with which the patient has coverage or information about the subscriber associated with the additional health plan.\\n\\nFor example, if you submit a COB check for a dependent to Cigna and Stedi finds additional coverage through Aetna, the `benefitsRelatedEntity` would include subscriber details for the Aetna plan.\",\"items\":{\"properties\":{\"entityFirstname\":{\"description\":\"The entity's first name, when the entity is a subscriber.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"entityIdentification\":{\"description\":\"Code identifying the type of `benefitsInformation.entityIdentificationValue`. This can be either `MI` - Member ID or `PI` - Payer ID.\",\"enum\":[\"MI\",\"PI\"],\"type\":\"string\"},\"entityIdentificationValue\":{\"description\":\"The identification number for the entity, qualified by the code in `entityIdentification`. The ID returned in this property is proprietary to our COB check product, so you can't use it as the Payer ID for eligibility checks or other API requests to Stedi. It likely doesn't match the Payer IDs listed in the [Payer Network](https://www.stedi.com/healthcare/network).\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"entityIdentifier\":{\"description\":\"Identifies the type of entity. This can either be a type of payer or the subscriber associated with the COB coverage.\\n\\nNote that Stedi uses `Payer` either when there is no COB instance or when Stedi is unable to determine primacy.\",\"enum\":[\"Insured or Subscriber\",\"Payer\",\"Primary Payer\",\"Secondary Payer\",\"Tertiary Payer\"],\"type\":\"string\"},\"entityLastname\":{\"description\":\"The entity's last name, when the entity is a subscriber.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"entityMiddlename\":{\"description\":\"The entity's middle name or initial, when the entity is a subscriber.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"entityName\":{\"description\":\"The payer's business name, when the entity is a overview of the COB response. It indicates whether there is a coverage overlap, whether that overlap creates a coordination of benefits instance, and whether Stedi was able to identify payer primacy (when a COB instance exists).\",\"properties\":{\"benefitOverlap\":{\"description\":\"If set to `true`, the COB response contains benefits overlap. A benefits overlap indicates that the patient has active coverage from two or more payers for the same service type code, including the subtypes of medical coverage.\",\"type\":\"boolean\"},\"classification\":{\"description\":\"The classification for the benefits that have been discovered in the COB response. Stedi returns one of the following values:\\n    - `CobInstanceExistsPrimacyDetermined`: COB Instance Exists and Primacy was determined\\n    - `CobInstanceExistsPrimacyUndetermined`: COB Instance Exists and Primacy was NOT determined\\n    - `CoverageOverlapNoBenefitOverlap`: Coverage Overlap detected with no Benefit Overlap\\n    - `CoverageOverlapExistsNotSubjectToCob`: Coverage Overlap exists and is not subject to COB\\n    - `MemberFoundNoCob`: Member found, no COB found\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"coverageOverlap\":{\"description\":\"If set to `true`, the COB response contains a coverage overlap, meaning that the patient has active coverage with two or more payers during the service date submitted in the COB request.\\n    - Coverage overlap can be for coverages from the same payer if the member ID is different between the two coverages.\\n    - A coverage overlap is necessary for a COB instance to exist.\\n    - A coverage overlap can exist without there being a COB instance if either of the two coverages is not subject to COB for any reason.\",\"type\":\"boolean\"},\"instanceExists\":{\"description\":\"If set to `true`, the COB response contains at least one coordination of benefits instance.\",\"type\":\"boolean\"},\"primacyDetermined\":{\"description\":\"If set to `true`, Stedi was able to determine the primary payer for the patient.
